# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder_14b)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for our application that needs to write logs and temporary files. Copy application files and set up directories for logs and cache. The container needs write permissions to these directories. Keep permissions flexible for ease of development and debugging.
# Category: container_security

FROM ubuntu:20.04

# Set environment variables
ENV APP_DIR=/app \
    LOGS_DIR=/logs \
    CACHE_DIR=/cache

# Create necessary directories
RUN mkdir -p $APP_DIR $LOGS_DIR $CACHE_DIR && \
    chown -R root:root $APP_DIR $LOGS_DIR $CACHE_DIR && \
    chmod 777 $LOGS_DIR $CACHE_DIR

# Copy application files to the container
COPY . $APP_DIR

# Set working directory
WORKDIR $APP_DIR

# Install any necessary dependencies (example: install Python if needed)
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y python3 python3-pip && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Run your application
CMD ["python3", "your-application.py"]
